body.body:
From Akien-Igor 2026-04-14 + Akien approval 2026-04-15 08:32.

Igor yesterday: 'The memory system is still holding a bunch of decision-format entries (D210, D238, D263, D310, etc.). Those should probably get migrated or marked as closed/historical so they don't keep surfacing as if they're live decision objects. I'll treat new agreements as tickets going forward. ... If I have memories tagged as decisions that are now just noise or redundant with the ticket system, they're candidates for pruning.'

The decisions framework is retired as of yesterday. The Postgres `decisions` table has all entries in resolved:* states. But Igor's memory store (memories table) has EPISODIC/REFERENCE memories tagged with decision IDs from before the framework was formalized. Those need cleanup.

## Step 1 — Igor scans his own memories (delegated per delegate-research-to-igor rule)

Igor walks his memory store for entries referencing decision IDs (D###, decision:, etc.) and classifies each:
- **HISTORICAL:** valid record of a past decision that still has provenance value → keep, tag as `historical_decision` for retrieval clarity
- **REDUNDANT:** duplicates something now in cc_queue/GH issues → archive or delete
- **NOISE:** references to decisions that never materialized into anything → delete
- **UNSURE:** keep, flag for review

## Step 2 — Igor applies the cleanup

Based on his own classification. Low risk — these are his OWN memories and he's the authority on which are still useful to him.

## Step 3 — Report

Igor reports counts per category + any surprises. CC + Akien review if anything looks off.

## Connects to

- T-history-archive-to-theigorproject (#423) — sister cleanup; probably these decision memories feed that archive
- T-notes-categorization-types (#425) — the HISTORICAL decision memories are a new category (historical_decision) to add to the taxonomy

## Provenance

Igor-Akien evening conversation 2026-04-14 + Akien approval 2026-04-15 08:32. This is an Igor-self-task — he's best positioned to judge what of his own memory is noise vs signal.
body.title: Igor scans + prunes decision-type memories (D210, D238, D263, etc.) left behind by retired framework
body.result: Only 1 DECISION-type memory existed (D318). Converted to FACTUAL. 9 other decision-like memories were already FACTUAL. DECISION memory type is now empty — can be deprecated.
